items.title: Denmark: Government formation faces prolonged negotiations – Nordea
items.summary: Nordea’s Group Chief Economist Helge J. Pedersen notes that the Danish parliamentary election has produced a highly fragmented Folketing, with 12 parties entering parliament and no majority for either the red or blue bloc. The Moderates, led by Lars Løkke Rasmussen, now hold the balance of power.

items.title: Oil stabilizes after losses as US-Iran talks ease supply fears, inventories rise
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) trades around $88.20 on Wednesday at the time of writing, up 0.40% on the day, attempting to stabilize after a recent pullback.

items.title: Fed: Oil shock complicates rate path – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir argue that the Federal Reserve (Fed) faces conflicting signals as the Iran conflict drives an Oil shock.

items.title: USD: Upside risk persists in conflict-driven markets – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes that global risk sentiment has improved as markets position for a potential conflict resolution involving Iran, with DXY consolidating below 100.00.

items.title: GBP: BoE hawkish shift supports Pound – UOB
items.summary: UOB economist Lee Sue Ann highlights a hawkish pivot by the Bank of England (BoE), with the Bank Rate held at 3.75% and a unanimous 9–0 vote. The report removes prior expectations for three 2026 cuts, now forecasting the GBP Repo Rate steady at 3.75% through 4Q26 as inflation risks dominate.

items.title: Oil: Supply shock deepens as Hormuz stays constrained – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities’ Senior Commodity Strategist Ryan McKay warns that Oil markets face escalating tightness as flows through the Strait of Hormuz remain severely restricted and Gulf production cuts exceed 10m b/d.

items.title: Germany: Rebound risks grow on Ifo slump – ING
items.summary: ING’s Carsten Brzeski warns that Germany’s long-awaited cyclical rebound has been dented after the Ifo index fell sharply in March, with expectations suffering their worst hit since the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

items.title: UK: Energy scenarios shape BoE outlook – ING
items.summary: ING’s James Smith outlines scenarios for UK inflation, with current energy prices implying a brief 4% peak in autumn and ING’s base case of easing disruption pointing to a 3.5% peak in September.

items.title: Germany: War risks darken Ifo outlook – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Chief Economist Dr. Jörg Krämer warns that the sharp March drop in the German Ifo Business Climate Index reflects rising war-related risks rather than current damage.

items.title: BoE's Greene: There is a risk inflation expectations will rise
items.summary: Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee member Megan Greene said on Wednesday that if their inflation forecasts are right, there is a risk inflation expectations will rise, per Reuters.

items.title: Iran sees truce and talks as not viable in current conditions – Fars
items.summary: Citing informed sources on the matter, Iran's Fars news agency reported on Wednesday that Tehran doesn't see truce and talks as viable in current conditions, despite the United States' increased efforts to establish a ceasefire and begin direct negotiations.

items.title: BoC: Dovish stance versus market pricing – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategist Andrew Kelvin notes that the Bank of Canada (BoC) is stressing downside risks to growth and appears willing to look through an energy-driven inflation shock.
